title: 10 best herbal tinctures for gastritis
intro_desc: Herbal tinctures for gastritis are concentrated liquid extracts of herbs that are used to treat gastritis, a condition characterized by inflammation of the stomach lining. These tinctures offer a natural and effective way to alleviate gastritis symptoms such as stomach pain, bloating, and nausea. Examples of herbal tinctures that are beneficial for gastritis include chamomile, licorice root, marshmallow root, and slippery elm. Chamomile has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in the stomach, while licorice root and marshmallow root can protect and soothe the stomach lining. Slippery elm, on the other hand, can help reduce stomach acid production. By using these herbal tinctures, individuals with gastritis can experience relief from their symptoms and improve their quality of life, without relying on harsh pharmaceutical drugs that may cause unwanted side effects.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe vera tincture helps with gastritis because it contains anti-inflammatory and healing properties that can soothe the digestive tract and reduce inflammation in the stomach lining. The tincture is made from the inner fillet of the aloe vera leaf, which is rich in vitamins, minerals, and enzymes that can aid in digestion and alleviate symptoms of gastritis such as pain, bloating, and nausea. Additionally, aloe vera tincture has been shown to have antibacterial properties that can help eliminate the H. pylori bacteria, a common cause of gastritis. Its mucilaginous properties can also protect the stomach lining from acid and irritation, providing relief from gastritis symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Aloe vera tincture can reduce inflammation in the stomach lining, providing relief from gastritis symptoms, due to the presence of compounds like bradykinase, salicylic acid, and various antioxidants.
Mucosal protective: It forms a protective layer on the gastric mucosa, preventing acid-induced damage and promoting healing, thanks to the polysaccharide content, mainly acemannan.
Antisecretory: Aloe vera tincture inhibits gastric acid secretion, which helps alleviate gastritis symptoms, as a result of its anthraquinone content, such as aloin and emodin.

Antibacterial: It combats Helicobacter pylori, a primary cause of gastritis, by inhibiting its growth and adhesion to the gastric mucosa, owing to the presence of various antibacterial agents like lupeol and lectins.

Gastroprotective: Aloe vera tincture promotes gastric mucosal integrity and overall stomach health, reducing the risk of gastritis, due to its antioxidant and nutrient content, including vitamins, minerals, and enzymes.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of the aloe vera plant contain a gel-like substance that is rich in anti-inflammatory compounds, making it effective in reducing inflammation in the stomach lining caused by gastritis.
Seeds: Aloe vera seeds contain beneficial oils that have been shown to have gastroprotective effects, making them useful for treating gastritis.

Stems: The stems of the aloe vera plant contain a sap that is rich in anthraquinones, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving effects, making them useful for treating gastritis symptoms.
Flowers: The flowers of the aloe vera plant contain nectar that is rich in antioxidants, which can help reduce oxidative stress in the stomach and alleviate gastritis symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh aloe vera leaves and rinse them thoroughly with distilled water.
Slice the aloe vera leaves thinly and combine them with high-proof grain alcohol in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for four to six weeks, shaking the jar daily.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container discarding the solids.
Transfer the filtered tincture to amber glass bottles and store them in the refrigerator at 40°f for up to two years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wormwood tincture helps with gastritis because it contains active compounds, such as absinthin and azulene, which have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. These components can help reduce inflammation in the stomach lining, providing relief from gastritis symptoms like pain, bloating, and nausea. Moreover, wormwood has been shown to stimulate the production of digestive juices, enhancing digestion and absorption of nutrients, while also inhibiting the growth of harmful bacteria in the gut that can contribute to gastritis. Overall, wormwood tincture can be an effective natural remedy for managing gastritis and promoting overall gastrointestinal health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Wormwood tincture's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce gastric inflammation, a key factor in gastritis, due to the presence of compounds like sesquiterpene lactones and flavonoids.

Antimicrobial: The antimicrobial properties of wormwood tincture assist in eliminating Helicobacter pylori, a bacterium commonly linked to gastritis, thanks to its active constituents, such as artemisinin and thujone.

Carminative: Wormwood tincture's carminative properties help alleviate gastritis symptoms by reducing gastric spasms and promoting the expulsion of intestinal gas, attributed to compounds like chamazulene and camphene.

Antioxidant: The antioxidant properties of wormwood tincture help protect the gastric mucosa from oxidative damage, supporting gastritis treatment, due to the presence of active compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids.

Digestive stimulant: Wormwood tincture's ability to stimulate digestion aids in improving nutrient absorption and reducing gastric acid production, thus benefiting gastritis treatment, owing to the active constituents, such as absinthin and anabsinthin.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in anti-inflammatory compounds, leaves are used to soothe gastritis-related inflammation.
Flowers: Containing calming properties, flowers help reduce gastritis-induced stomach spasms.
Seeds: Seeds are abundant in essential oils, providing relief for gastritis-related pain and discomfort.
Stems: High in bitter compounds, stems stimulate digestion and alleviate gastritis symptoms.
Roots: Packed with therapeutic constituents, roots have been used to treat gastritis and support overall gastrointestinal health.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried wormwood leaves and flowers according to recommended dosages for tincture making.
Chop the wormwood material into small pieces to increase surface area for extraction.
Combine chopped wormwood with high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar at a 1:5 ratio by weight.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for four to six weeks, shaking daily, to allow for maceration and tincturing.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass bottle, discarding solids, and store in a dark cool place until ready for use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Common barberry tincture helps with gastritis because it contains berberine, an alkaloid that has been shown to have antibacterial properties. This compound may help to eliminate the Helicobacter pylori bacteria, which is often responsible for causing gastritis. Additionally, barberry has anti-inflammatory effects, which can help to reduce inflammation in the stomach lining and provide relief from gastritis symptoms such as pain, bloating, and nausea. Barberry may also help to stimulate the production of digestive juices, promoting healthy digestion and supporting overall gastrointestinal health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Common barberry tincture can reduce inflammation in the stomach lining, which is important for gastritis as it is primarily characterized by inflammation. The active constituents responsible for this property include berberine and oxycanthine.
Astringent: Barberry tincture helps to tighten and tone the mucous membranes of the stomach, providing relief from gastritis symptoms. This is due to the tannins present in the tincture.
Antimicrobial: Common barberry tincture can help eliminate the H. pylori bacteria, a common cause of gastritis. The active constituent responsible for this property is berberine.
Antispasmodic: Barberry tincture can help relieve stomach spasms and cramps associated with gastritis. This is due to the alkaloids present in the tincture.

Amphoteric: Barberry tincture can help balance stomach acid levels, either increasing or decreasing them as needed, which is important for gastritis as it can be caused by both high and low stomach acid levels. The active constituents responsible for this property include berberine and isoquinoline alkaloids.
remedy_parts: Roots: Barberry roots are commonly used in medicinal tinctures for gastritis due to their high berberine content, which has anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties.
Bark: The bark of the barberry plant is used in tinctures for gastritis because it contains alkaloids that can help reduce inflammation and soothe digestive issues.
Berries: Barberry berries are rich in antioxidants and vitamins, making them a valuable addition to tinctures for gastritis, as they can help protect the stomach lining and promote healing.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh berberis vulagris roots and stems, wash them gently with clean water.
Chop the harvested material into small pieces, making sure they are dry and free of debris.
Combine one part chopped barberry root and stem material with one part 95% ethanol in a glass jar.
Seal the jar tightly, label it, and store it in a dark place for four to six weeks for maceration.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container, squeezing the solids gently.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: White tea tincture helps with gastritis because it contains anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce inflammation in the stomach lining, providing relief from gastritis symptoms. It is rich in antioxidants that can help protect the stomach from damage caused by free radicals. White tea tincture also has antibacterial properties that can help eliminate harmful bacteria in the stomach, such as Helicobacter pylori, which can cause gastritis. Additionally, it can help stimulate the production of digestive enzymes, improving digestion and absorption of nutrients, while soothing the stomach and promoting overall gut health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: White tea tincture can reduce inflammation in the stomach lining, providing relief from gastritis symptoms, due to the presence of flavonoids such as catechins.
Antioxidant: Its high antioxidant content, including epigallocatechin gallate (EGCG), helps protect the stomach lining from damage caused by free radicals, which can exacerbate gastritis.
Antibacterial: White tea tincture can inhibit the growth of Helicobacter pylori, a bacterium commonly associated with gastritis, thanks to its active compounds like tannins and polyphenols.
Gastroprotective: White tea tincture can help strengthen and protect the stomach lining, reducing the risk of gastritis, by stimulating the production of mucin, a component of the mucosal lining.

Antiulcer: White tea tincture may promote the healing of ulcers and prevent their recurrence, as its active constituents, such as catechins and theaflavins, can inhibit the breakdown of stomach lining and reduce acid production.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Contain high levels of antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in reducing gastric inflammation and promoting digestive health.
Buds: Rich in polyphenols, which have been shown to protect the gastric mucosa and reduce the risk of gastritis.
Roots: Contain high concentrations of antioxidants and have been traditionally used to treat gastrointestinal disorders, including gastritis.
Flowers: Have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making them useful for soothing and healing the digestive tract in cases of gastritis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh camellia sinensis leaves in early morning for maximum potency and freshness.
Wash the harvested leaves with filtered water to remove dirt and debris thoroughly.
Combine 1 part dried or 2 parts fresh camellia sinensis leaves with 2 parts vodka or grain alcohol in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture at room temperature for 2-6 weeks, shaking the jar daily, allowing flavors to meld and extract.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle, discarding solids and storing in cool dark place.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Caraway tincture helps with gastritis because it contains anti-inflammatory and carminative properties that can soothe the digestive system and reduce inflammation in the stomach lining. Its active compounds, such as carvone and limonene, can help relieve symptoms of gastritis, such as bloating, abdominal pain, and nausea, by promoting the production of digestive enzymes and reducing spasms in the gastrointestinal tract. Additionally, caraway tincture has been shown to have antibacterial properties that can help eliminate harmful bacteria in the stomach, such as Helicobacter pylori, which can contribute to gastritis. Overall, caraway tincture can provide a natural and effective way to manage gastritis symptoms and support overall digestive health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Caraway tincture's anti-inflammatory property helps reduce gastritis-related inflammation, soothing the stomach lining, thanks to compounds like limonene and carvone.

Antispasmodic: Its antispasmodic property alleviates gastritis-induced muscle spasms in the gastrointestinal tract, which can cause pain and discomfort, due to the presence of volatile oil components.
Carminative: Caraway tincture's carminative property helps expel gas and alleviate bloating, common symptoms of gastritis, by stimulating the release of gastric juices and bile.
Antimicrobial: The antimicrobial property of caraway tincture combats H. pylori bacteria, a common cause of gastritis, due to the presence of active compounds like carvacrol and thymol.

Digestive stimulant: Caraway tincture's digestive stimulant property promotes healthy digestion, enhancing nutrient absorption, and reducing gastritis symptoms, due to the presence of essential oils and flavonoids.
remedy_parts: Seeds: Caraway seeds are commonly used in making medicinal tinctures for gastritis due to their carminative and antispasmodic properties, which help relieve symptoms such as bloating, gas, and stomach cramps.

Rhizomes: The rhizomes of caraway plants are used in tinctures for gastritis because of their anti-inflammatory and soothing effects on the digestive tract, which can help reduce inflammation and irritation.

Leaves: Caraway leaves contain volatile oils and flavonoids that contribute to their calming and protective effects on the gastrointestinal lining, making them a valuable addition to medicinal tinctures for gastritis.
Flowers: Caraway flowers are rich in antioxidants and have been shown to help alleviate gastrointestinal issues, making them a suitable component for medicinal tinctures aimed at treating gastritis.

Roots: The roots of caraway plants are used in tinctures for gastritis due to their ability to stimulate digestive juices, which aids in better nutrient absorption and helps maintain a healthy gastrointestinal system.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried carum carvi seeds at peak potency for optimal flavor and aroma extraction.
Grind the harvested carum carvi seeds into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Combine 100g of the powdered carum carvi with 500ml of high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar, ensuring adequate coverage.
Store the mixture at room temperature for 2-4 weeks, shaking the jar daily to facilitate extraction and maceration.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a separate container, discarding the solids and preserving the resulting tincture.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Common centaury tincture helps with gastritis because it contains bitter compounds that stimulate the production of digestive enzymes and bile, which can improve overall digestion and reduce inflammation in the stomach lining. Its anti-inflammatory properties may also help alleviate symptoms such as pain, bloating, and nausea associated with gastritis. Additionally, common centaury has been shown to have a protective effect on the mucous membrane of the stomach, which can help prevent further damage and promote healing.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Centaury tincture helps reduce inflammation in the stomach lining, providing relief from gastritis symptoms, due to its active constituents such as bitter glycosides and flavonoids.
Astringent: It helps tighten and tone the mucous membranes of the stomach, protecting them from irritation and inflammation, due to tannins present in centaury.
Choleretic: Centaury tincture stimulates the production of bile, aiding in digestion and reducing the burden on the stomach, thanks to its active constituents like sesquiterpene lactones.

remedy_parts: Rhizomes: The underground stem of common centaury contains bitter compounds that can stimulate digestion and reduce inflammation in the stomach lining, making it useful for treating gastritis.

Leaves: The leaves of common centaury are rich in antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe and heal the stomach lining, making them a valuable addition to medicinal tinctures for gastritis.
Flowers: The flowers of common centaury contain flavonoids that have been shown to protect the stomach lining from damage, making them a useful ingredient in medicinal tinctures for gastritis.

Seeds: The seeds of common centaury contain essential oils and tannins that can help reduce inflammation and irritation in the stomach, making them a valuable component of medicinal tinctures for gastritis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried centaurium erythraea leaves and stems in quantities of one pound for every cup of menstruum required.
Chop the plant material into small pieces using scissors or a mortar and pestle to increase surface area for extraction.
Combine the chopped plant material with 100 proof vodka or everclear as the menstruum at a ratio of 1:5 parts plant to solvent.
Steep the mixture in a glass jar or container with a tight-fitting lid for four weeks, shaking daily, and store in a cool dark place.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container, pressing on solids to extract as much liquid as possible.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peruvian bark tincture helps with gastritis because it contains powerful anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which can alleviate pain and inflammation in the stomach lining. The herb's active compounds, such as tannins and alkaloids, have been shown to protect the stomach from damage caused by gastritis, and promote the healing of stomach ulcers. Additionally, Peruvian bark tincture can help reduce acid production in the stomach, providing relief from symptoms such as bloating, nausea, and heartburn. Its soothing effects on the digestive system can also help improve overall digestion and absorption of nutrients, further supporting gastritis recovery.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Peruvian bark tincture contains alkaloids such as cinchonine and quinine, which help reduce inflammation in the stomach lining, providing relief from gastritis symptoms.
Antacid: The bark extract has been shown to have antacid properties, neutralizing stomach acid and helping to prevent the irritation and inflammation associated with gastritis.
Protective: Peruvian bark tincture contains tannins that form a protective layer over the stomach lining, reducing exposure to harmful digestive acids and aiding in gastritis treatment.
Pain-relieving: The alkaloids in Peruvian bark tincture, such as cinchonine and quinine, act as analgesics, helping to alleviate pain and discomfort caused by gastritis.
Antimicrobial: The tincture has antimicrobial properties, which can help eliminate Helicobacter pylori, a common cause of gastritis, thus aiding in the treatment of the condition.
remedy_parts: Bark: The bark of the Peruvian bark plant contains quinine, which has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in the stomach lining caused by gastritis.
Roots: The roots of the Peruvian bark plant contain alkaloids that can help reduce stomach acid production, providing relief from gastritis symptoms.

remedy_recipe: Harvest cinchona officinalis barks at peak potency in june and july, drying them immediately.
Chop dried cinchona officinalis barks into small pieces, then combine with 50% vodka by weight.
Store the mixture in a glass container for four weeks, shaking daily for 15 minutes.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter, discarding solids after two hours.
Transfer the tincture to dark glass bottles and store at room temperature for up to five years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Coptis chinensis tincture helps with gastritis because it contains berberine, a compound with potent anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. Gastritis is often caused by the bacterium Helicobacter pylori, and berberine can help eradicate this infection. Additionally, Coptis chinensis has been shown to reduce inflammation in the stomach lining, providing relief from symptoms such as pain, bloating, and nausea. Its ability to inhibit the production of acid in the stomach also makes it an effective remedy for gastritis. Overall, Coptis chinensis tincture can help alleviate the symptoms of gastritis and promote the healing of the stomach lining.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Coptis chinensis tincture can reduce inflammation in the stomach lining, which is important for gastritis as it is a primary cause of the condition. The anti-inflammatory properties come from its active constituents, such as berberine and palmatine.

Antimicrobial: Coptis chinensis tincture has antimicrobial properties that can help eliminate the H. pylori bacteria, a common cause of gastritis. The antimicrobial properties are attributed to its alkaloids, including berberine and jatrorrhizine.

Antioxidant: Coptis chinensis tincture has antioxidant properties that can help protect the stomach lining from damage caused by free radicals. This is important for gastritis as it can help reduce inflammation and promote healing. The antioxidant properties are due to its phenolic compounds, such as coptisine and epiberberine.

Anti-secretory: Coptis chinensis tincture can inhibit gastric acid secretion, which is important for gastritis as excessive acid production can further irritate and damage the stomach lining. The anti-secretory properties are attributed to its alkaloids, particularly berberine.

Analgesic: Coptis chinensis tincture has analgesic properties that can help alleviate pain associated with gastritis. This is important for improving the quality of life for individuals with gastritis. The analgesic properties are due to its alkaloids, including berberine and palmatine.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Coptis chinensis are commonly used in medicinal tinctures for gastritis due to their high alkaloid content, which has anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties.
Rhizomes: The rhizomes of this plant are rich in berberine, a compound known for its ability to protect the gastric mucosa and reduce inflammation.
Flowers: Although less commonly used, the flowers of Coptis chinensis contain flavonoids that have been shown to have gastroprotective effects, making them useful in treating gastritis.
Seeds: The seeds of this plant contain various beneficial compounds, including alkaloids and saponins, which can help reduce inflammation and protect the stomach lining in cases of gastritis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality coptis chinensis roots at optimal maturity for best bioactive compounds retention.
Clean and slice the harvested roots into small pieces, ensuring minimal exposure to air and moisture.
Combine 50g of sliced coptis roots with 500ml of high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar, sealing tightly.
Store the jar in a dark place at room temperature for 14 days, shaking daily during the maceration period.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into another clean container, discarding the solids and preserving the tincture.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric tincture helps with gastritis because it contains curcumin, a powerful anti-inflammatory compound that can help reduce inflammation in the stomach lining. Gastritis is often caused by an H. pylori bacterial infection or excessive use of NSAIDs, both of which can cause inflammation. The curcumin in turmeric tincture can help inhibit the growth of H. pylori and reduce inflammation, providing relief from gastritis symptoms such as pain, bloating, and nausea. Additionally, turmeric has been shown to stimulate the production of bile, which can aid in digestion and further support gastritis treatment.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Turmeric tincture's anti-inflammatory property, due to curcumin, helps reduce inflammation in the stomach lining, a key factor in gastritis.
Gastroprotective: The gastroprotective effect of turmeric tincture, attributed to curcumin and other curcuminoids, shields the stomach lining from damage, beneficial for gastritis.
Antioxidant: Turmeric tincture's antioxidant property, owed to curcumin, neutralizes free radicals, minimizing oxidative stress in the stomach, which is prevalent in gastritis.
Antisecretory: The antisecretory property of turmeric tincture, attributed to curcumin, decreases gastric acid secretion, alleviating gastritis symptoms.
Antimicrobial: Turmeric tincture's antimicrobial property, due to curcumin and other compounds, combats Helicobacter pylori, a common cause of gastritis.
remedy_parts: Rhizomes: Turmeric rhizomes are commonly used to make medicinal tinctures for gastritis due to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help reduce inflammation and protect the stomach lining.
Roots: Turmeric roots contain curcumin, which has been shown to help alleviate gastritis symptoms by reducing inflammation and promoting the healing of the stomach lining.
Leaves: Turmeric leaves have been used in traditional medicine for their antimicrobial properties, which may help combat H. pylori, a common cause of gastritis.
Flowers: Turmeric flowers contain essential oils and curcumin, which have been found to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, making them useful for treating gastritis.
Seeds: Turmeric seeds have been used in traditional medicine for their carminative properties, which can help reduce gastric discomfort and promote digestion.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh turmeric roots with a total of 20-30 grams or dried powder equivalent for tincture production.
Chop the harvested turmeric roots into small pieces and soak them in 80% ethanol vodka for 4-6 weeks.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass jar, squeezing out as much liquid as possible from the solids.
Transfer the extracted liquid to a dark glass bottle, diluting it with distilled water to a 20% concentration and store it in a cool place for at least 2 weeks.
Filter the tincture through a coffee filter or paper towel into a clean glass bottle, discarding any sediment and label the product accurately.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wild yam tincture helps with gastritis because it contains anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties that can soothe the stomach lining and reduce inflammation. The herb's active compounds, such as diosgenin, have been shown to protect the mucous membrane of the stomach, preventing the production of excess stomach acid that can cause gastritis. Additionally, wild yam tincture can help alleviate symptoms of gastritis, such as abdominal pain, bloating, and nausea, by promoting the healthy functioning of the digestive system. Its demulcent properties can also help create a protective coating on the stomach lining, further reducing irritation and promoting healing.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Wild yam tincture can reduce inflammation in the stomach lining, which is important for gastritis as it is primarily characterized by inflammation. The anti-inflammatory properties come from the steroidal saponins, such as diosgenin, present in wild yam.

Mucilaginous: Wild yam tincture forms a protective layer on the stomach lining, helping to prevent irritation and further inflammation. This is important for gastritis as it can help to alleviate symptoms and promote healing.

Antispasmodic: Wild yam tincture can help to relax the smooth muscles of the gastrointestinal tract, reducing spasms and cramping. This is important for gastritis as it can help to alleviate pain and discomfort.

Demulcent: Wild yam tincture has a soothing effect on mucous membranes, which can help to reduce irritation and inflammation in the stomach. This is important for gastritis as it can help to alleviate symptoms and promote healing.

remedy_parts: Roots: The wild yam roots are commonly used in making medicinal tinctures for gastritis due to their anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties that help soothe the digestive tract.
Rhizomes: The rhizomes of wild yam contain compounds that can help reduce gastric acid production, making them useful for treating gastritis symptoms.
Leaves: Wild yam leaves are rich in flavonoids that have been shown to protect the stomach lining, making them a valuable addition to medicinal tinctures for gastritis.
Flowers: The flowers of wild yam contain essential oils that have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help alleviate gastritis symptoms.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh dioscorea villosa tubers in the fall or spring ensuring they are free from any signs of disease or pests.
Clean and dry the harvested tubers gently removing any dirt debris or excess moisture with a soft brush.
Chop the dried tubers into small pieces measuring about 10-15 mm and store them in an airtight container for up to six months.
Mix one part of the chopped tuber material with one part of vodka or other high-proof alcohol in a clean glass jar and seal tightly.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for at least four weeks shaking the jar every few days during that time ensuring maximum tincture strength.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat gastritis with herbal tinctures is by using chamomile, marshmallow root, and licorice root extracts. These herbs have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce gastric inflammation, promote healing of the stomach lining, and alleviate symptoms such as pain and bloating. Mix 10-15 drops of each tincture in a glass of water and drink it three times a day, 20 minutes before meals. Always consult a healthcare professional before starting any new treatment.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to gastritis that are treated with herbal tinctures include acid reflux, stomach ulcers, and irritable bowel syndrome. Herbal tinctures, such as those made from chamomile, licorice root, and marshmallow, can help reduce inflammation, soothe the digestive tract, and alleviate symptoms like pain, bloating, and acidity. These natural remedies are often used as an alternative or complement to conventional treatments and may provide a safer, gentler option for those with sensitive stomachs or allergies to over-the-counter medications. However, it is important to consult with a healthcare provider before starting any new treatment regimen.
